title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: A landmark work that brings cosmology and theoretical physics to everyday readers — exploring the Big Bang, black holes, and the very nature of time and space.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A magical story about Santiago, an Andalusian shepherd boy who yearns to travel in search of a worldly treasure as extravagant as any ever found.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship that teaches programmers how to write clean, readable, and maintainable code.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: A proven framework for improving every day. Learn how tiny changes in behavior can lead to remarkable results in your life.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: A personal voyage through the universe, exploring the origins of life, the nature of science, and humanity's place in the cosmos.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: David Thomas
description: A guide for programmers who want to become more effective, covering topics from personal responsibility to career development.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A brief history of humankind that explores how Homo sapiens came to dominate the earth and what the future holds for our species.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian masterpiece set in a totalitarian society where Big Brother watches your every move and the truth is whatever the Party says it is.

title: You Don't Know JS
author: Kyle Simpson
description: A deep dive into the core mechanisms of the JavaScript language, covering scope, closures, types, and asynchronous programming.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A story of the fabulously wealthy Jay Gatsby and his love for the beautiful Daisy Buchanan, set against the backdrop of the Jazz Age.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: A groundbreaking work that introduces the gene-centered view of evolution and the concept of the meme as a unit of cultural transmission.

title: JavaScript: The Good Parts
author: Douglas Crockford
description: A concise guide to the most reliable and elegant features of JavaScript, helping developers write better and more maintainable code.
